questions.context: Lines AB and CD are parallel. EF is a transversal. Angle AEG = (5x − 20)° and angle CGF = (3x + 40)°, where G is the intersection of EF with another line.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
Given that AEG and CGF are corresponding angles, work out the value of x.
Give a reason for your answer.

markScheme.desc: Reason: corresponding angles are equal between parallel lines

workingSteps: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the size of angle AEG.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Work out the co-interior angle to angle AEG.
workingSteps: Co-interior angles between parallel lines sum to 180°

questions.context: In triangle PQR, PQ = PR. The exterior angle at Q is 118°.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
Work out angle PQR.
Give a reason for your answer.

markScheme.desc: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°

workingSteps: PQR + exterior angle at Q = 180° (angles on straight line)

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Work out angle QPR.
Give reasons for each step.

markScheme.desc: PQ = PR so angle PQR = angle PRQ (base angles of isosceles triangle)

workingSteps: PQ = PR means triangle PQR is isosceles
Base angles are equal: angle PRQ = angle PQR = 62°

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Verify that the exterior angle at Q equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

markScheme.desc: Sum of non-adjacent interior angles = PRQ + QPR = 62 + 56

workingSteps: Non-adjacent interior angles: PRQ = 62° and QPR = 56°
This confirms the exterior angle theorem.
